title: Day 1: Claude Setup & Your AI Personality Profile
problemStatement:
Welcome to **Day 1** of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

**Theme:** Phase 1 — Foundation & Prompt Engineering (Orientation)

**Today's focus:** Set up Claude on your desktop and create your personal AI Personality Profile.

**Tasks:**
1. Install and explore Claude on desktop (and/or web).
2. Run the “AI Personality Profile” prompt to generate your personalized profile and cinematic AI portrait.
3. Share your profile + portrait on LinkedIn to kick off your 60-day journey.

**Submission:** Share a Claude conversation link, artifact, or short doc that shows your work.

**Note:** Real content from our team launches before June 1, 2026.
learningObjectives: Apply Day 1 concepts to your real role or studies
Build repeatable prompts you can reuse after the challenge
Practice safe, reviewable AI workflows
linkedinTemplate:
Day 1/60 of #60DayClaudeChallenge with @ABtalks 🚀

Today I set up Claude and generated my personal AI Personality Profile — my AI working style, strengths, and the kind of AI user I’m becoming.

60 days. 60 real projects. Building in public.

#60DayClaudeChallenge #ABtalks
solutionApproach: Submit a valid Claude share or artifact URL, plus your LinkedIn post URL when prompted.

dayContent.title: Claude Setup & AI Personality Profile
dayContent.tagline: Set up your workspace and meet your AI self

learning.summary: Two things on Day 1: get Claude set up so you can use it daily, and run a fun first prompt that gives you a cinematic “AI Personality Profile” — a great first post for your 60-day journey.

bullets.label: Desktop access
bullets.text: install Claude on desktop and pin it to your dock/taskbar for daily use.

bullets.label: Web access
bullets.text: sign in to claude.ai in your browser and explore the chat, Projects, and Artifacts.

bullets.label: Workspace hygiene
bullets.text: create your first Project (“60 Days of AI”) so all your challenge chats stay organized.

bullets.label: First prompt
bullets.text: use the AI Personality Profile prompt to generate a personalized profile + cinematic portrait.

task.title: Your Task
steps: Install Claude on your desktop (link above) and sign in.
Create a Claude Project named “60 Days of AI” — you’ll save every challenge chat here.
Open a new chat and paste the AI Personality Profile prompt below. Replace the brackets with details that fit you.
Let Claude generate your profile AND the cinematic AI portrait in the same response.
Save the result to your portfolio and share it on LinkedIn to mark Day 1.

dayContent.promptTemplate:
Based on:
* how I use AI
* the prompts I ask
* my work, goals, mindset, and personality
* my strengths, weaknesses, curiosity, and communication style

Create a deeply personalized “AI Personality Profile” for me.

Make it accurate, modern, cinematic, internet-native, realistic but fun.

Include:
1. My AI working style
2. My strengths & weaknesses
3. What type of AI user I am
4. Best future career paths for me
5. My learning & decision-making style
6. What makes me different from most AI users
7. What I need to improve to become elite with AI
8. A fun AI title for me
9. A short cinematic AI character description

Then, generate a hyper-realistic cinematic AI portrait of me based on everything above.

The image should capture: startup founder / AI engineer energy, futuristic workspace & AI screens, coding + research vibe, dark cinematic lighting, modern Gen-Z builder aesthetic, realistic details with subtle cyberpunk accents.

IMPORTANT: don’t just write the image prompt — generate the actual image directly in chat. Make it professional, futuristic, cinematic, and LinkedIn-shareable.

engagement.description: Share your AI Personality Profile + cinematic portrait on LinkedIn. Tag @ABtalks and use #60DayClaudeChallenge to mark Day 1.

deliverable.description: Your AI Personality Profile + cinematic AI portrait

tool.name: Claude Desktop App
tool.description: Install Claude as a native desktop app on Mac, Windows, or Linux. It lives in your dock, opens faster than a browser tab, and supports global shortcuts.
tool.setupTitle: How to set up Claude on your desktop
setupSteps: Go to claude.ai/download in your browser.
Download the installer for your operating system (Mac / Windows / Linux).
Install it and sign in with the same account you use on claude.ai.
Pin Claude to your dock (Mac) or taskbar (Windows) so it’s one click away.
Open Claude → click “Projects” → create a new project named “60 Days of AI” to keep all your challenge chats together.

title: Day 2: What Is Prompt Engineering
problemStatement:
Welcome to **Day 2** of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

**Theme:** Phase 1 — Foundation & Prompt Engineering

**Today's focus:** Understand what Prompt Engineering really is, and feel the difference between a normal prompt and an engineered one.

**Tasks:**
1. Read the short intro to Prompt Engineering and the Normal vs Engineered comparison.
2. Rewrite one weak prompt of your own into a structured, professional version.
3. Post the before/after on LinkedIn.

**Submission:** Share a Claude conversation link, artifact, or short doc that shows your work.

**Note:** Real content from our team launches before June 1, 2026.
learningObjectives: Apply Day 2 concepts to your real role or studies
Build repeatable prompts you can reuse after the challenge
Practice safe, reviewable AI workflows
linkedinTemplate:
Day 2/60 of #60DayClaudeChallenge with @ABtalks 🚀

Today (Prompt Engineering): learned the difference between asking AI and *engineering* the prompt. One small shift, very different output.

What’s the laziest prompt you keep using? 👇

#60DayClaudeChallenge #ABtalks
solutionApproach: Submit a valid Claude share or artifact URL, plus your LinkedIn post URL when prompted.

dayContent.title: What Is Prompt Engineering
dayContent.tagline: The skill of talking to AI effectively

learning.summary: Prompt Engineering is the skill of writing smart, structured prompts to get more accurate answers, better creativity, expert-level outputs, fewer hallucinations, and faster workflows. Just like Googling well is a skill, prompting well is now a modern tech skill.

bullets.label: More accurate answers
bullets.text: structured prompts reduce guesswork and hallucination.

bullets.label: Expert-level outputs
bullets.text: give role + context and the model writes like a professional, not a generalist.

bullets.label: Faster workflow
bullets.text: one good prompt replaces five back-and-forth corrections.

bullets.label: Normal vs Engineered
bullets.text: “Make a website” vs “Act as a senior frontend engineer and build a responsive SaaS landing page using React + Tailwind with dark theme and smooth animations.”

task.title: Your Task
steps: Take a recent vague request you typed into Claude (or any AI).
Rewrite it as an engineered prompt: add role, context, instruction, and the format you want.
Run both versions in Claude and compare the outputs side by side.
Optional: use MetaPrompt to optimize it one more level and note what changed.

dayContent.promptTemplate:
Compare these two prompts on the same task:

NORMAL PROMPT:
[paste your lazy one-liner]

ENGINEERED PROMPT:
Role: You are a [profession] with strong experience.
Context: I need this for [audience] because [goal]. Constraints: [tone, length, must-include].
Instruction: [the single task].
Output Format: [bullets / table / 150-word paragraph].

Generate both outputs and label them clearly so I can compare.

engagement.description: Post your normal vs engineered prompt side by side and the two different outputs. Caption it: what surprised you most about the difference?

tool.name: MetaPrompt
tool.description: A free Chrome extension that rewrites your rough prompt into an expert, structured one with a single click, right inside Claude.
tool.setupTitle: How to set up MetaPrompt in Claude
setupSteps: Open the Chrome Web Store link below and click "Add to Chrome."
Pin the MetaPrompt icon to your toolbar (click the puzzle-piece icon, then the pin).
Go to claude.ai and type your rough prompt in the message box as usual.
Click the MetaPrompt button that appears near the input — it rewrites your prompt into a structured version.
Review the optimized prompt, then send it to Claude. Free plan gives 20 enhancements per month.

title: Day 3: Role-Based Prompting
problemStatement:
Welcome to **Day 3** of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

**Theme:** Phase 1 — Foundation & Prompt Engineering

**Today's focus:** Role-Based Prompting — turn Claude into any expert you need.

**Tasks:**
1. Take a real problem from your life or work.
2. Solve it using the Non-Tech and Tech role-based prompt templates.
3. Compare the answers and post the most useful one.

**Submission:** Share a Claude conversation link, artifact, or short doc that shows your work.

**Note:** Real content from our team launches before June 1, 2026.
learningObjectives: Apply Day 3 concepts to your real role or studies
Build repeatable prompts you can reuse after the challenge
Practice safe, reviewable AI workflows
linkedinTemplate:
Day 3/60 of #60DayClaudeChallenge with @ABtalks 🚀

Today (Role-Based Prompting): same question — wildly different answers when you change Claude’s persona.

Which “expert” would you want answering your toughest question? 👇

#60DayClaudeChallenge #ABtalks
solutionApproach: Submit a valid Claude share or artifact URL, plus your LinkedIn post URL when prompted.

dayContent.title: Role-Based Prompting
dayContent.tagline: Turn Claude into any expert you need

learning.summary: “Act-as” personas. By assigning Claude a specific professional identity — senior consultant, software developer, analyst — you instantly get sharper, domain-aware answers.

bullets.label: Personas shift output
bullets.text: a defined role changes vocabulary, depth, and the assumptions Claude makes.

bullets.label: Tone & style
bullets.text: name the communication style (direct, supportive, analytical) so the answer fits the moment.

bullets.label: Risks worth flagging
bullets.text: ask for 3 risks a less experienced person would miss — instantly more valuable answers.

bullets.label: Tech vs Non-Tech
bullets.text: use the right template depending on whether your question is technical or business-side.

task.title: Your Task
steps: Pick a real problem from your life or studies (e.g. “review my resume”, “design a database schema”).
Solve it once using the Non-Tech template (senior consultant persona).
Solve it again with the Tech template if it’s a technical problem (senior software developer persona).
Compare both answers — note what changed: tone, depth, risks called out.

dayContent.promptTemplate:
=== NON-TECH (general expert) ===
Act as a [senior role] with deep experience in [industry].
Your communication style is [direct / supportive / analytical].
Your audience is [who will read this].

Task: [what you need].
Give me your expert recommendation, then list the 3 risks a less experienced person would miss.

=== TECH (software/engineering) ===
Act as a senior software developer at a top tech company with experience building scalable, production-ready systems.
Communication style: professional, analytical, concise. Audience: a manager, tech lead, or stakeholder.

Task: [insert task]

Provide:
1. Your recommended solution or approach
2. Technical reasoning and implementation strategy
3. Key trade-offs, risks, or bottlenecks the team should consider
4. Three critical mistakes less experienced developers commonly miss

Focus on clean architecture, scalability, maintainability, performance, and real-world execution.

engagement.description: Share two answers to the same question generated with different role-based prompts. Which expert gave the most useful answer — and why?

title: Day 4: Chain-of-Thought Prompting
problemStatement:
Welcome to **Day 4** of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

**Theme:** Phase 1 — Foundation & Prompt Engineering

**Today's focus:** Chain-of-Thought — make Claude reason step-by-step on hard problems.

**Tasks:**
1. Pick a genuinely complex problem.
2. Solve it with a normal prompt, then again with the chain-of-thought template.
3. Compare reasoning quality and post your findings.

**Submission:** Share a Claude conversation link, artifact, or short doc that shows your work.

**Note:** Real content from our team launches before June 1, 2026.
learningObjectives: Apply Day 4 concepts to your real role or studies
Build repeatable prompts you can reuse after the challenge
Practice safe, reviewable AI workflows
linkedinTemplate:
Day 4/60 of #60DayClaudeChallenge with @ABtalks 🚀

Today (Chain-of-Thought): asked Claude to *think* before it answered. The difference on complex problems is huge.

What’s the hardest problem you’d let an AI reason through? 👇

#60DayClaudeChallenge #ABtalks
solutionApproach: Submit a valid Claude share or artifact URL, plus your LinkedIn post URL when prompted.

dayContent.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting
dayContent.tagline: Make Claude reason step-by-step for hard problems

learning.summary: Multi-step reasoning prompts. Instead of asking for an answer, you ask Claude to think through a problem in stages — understand, decompose, solve, verify, conclude. Dramatically better accuracy on anything complex.

bullets.label: Why it works
bullets.text: step-by-step structure forces the model to slow down and check itself.

bullets.label: Decompose
bullets.text: break a big problem into small named parts before solving any of them.

bullets.label: Verify
bullets.text: ask Claude to double-check its own logic before giving the final answer.

bullets.label: Final answer
bullets.text: always require a clearly labelled FINAL ANSWER so the takeaway is unmissable.

task.title: Your Task
steps: Pick a complex problem (a budget plan, a tricky decision, a multi-step calculation, an architecture choice).
Solve it once with a normal one-line prompt. Save the answer.
Solve it again using the chain-of-thought template below. Save the answer.
Compare both: which one would you actually trust to act on?

dayContent.promptTemplate:
I need help solving this complex problem:

[Insert problem/task]

Think through it step by step:

1. Understand the problem
   * What do we know?
   * What needs to be solved?

2. Break it into smaller parts
   * Explain each part briefly.

3. Solve systematically
   * Show your reasoning clearly.
   * Consider edge cases or alternatives if needed.

4. Verify the solution
   * Double-check the logic/calculations.

5. FINAL ANSWER
   * Give a concise, clearly labeled final answer with key takeaways.

Important:
* Don’t skip reasoning steps.
* Prioritize accuracy over speed.
* Mention uncertainties if any.

engagement.description: Share the normal answer vs the step-by-step answer to the same problem. Which one would you trust more, and why?

tool.name: Claude Usage Tracker
tool.description: A free Chrome extension that shows live token usage on claude.ai. Helpful here because chain-of-thought reasoning uses more tokens — you can see exactly how much “thinking” costs.
tool.setupTitle: How to set up the token counter in Claude
setupSteps: Open the Chrome Web Store link below and click "Add to Chrome."
Go to claude.ai and open any conversation.
The extension injects a live token count directly into the Claude interface — no setup or API key needed.
Watch the counter as you send messages; compare the count before and after a chain-of-thought prompt.

title: Day 5: Context Management — Build Your AI Engineer Roadmap
problemStatement:
Welcome to **Day 5** of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

**Theme:** Phase 1 — Foundation & Prompt Engineering

**Today's focus:** Use Claude as your personal mentor and generate a roadmap to become an AI Engineer (or any role you want).

**Tasks:**
1. Run the mentor/coach prompt with your real goal filled in.
2. Get back a structured roadmap with phases, projects, and a weekly plan.
3. Pick the first 3 actions and share your roadmap on LinkedIn.

**Submission:** Share a Claude conversation link, artifact, or short doc that shows your work.

**Note:** Real content from our team launches before June 1, 2026.
learningObjectives: Apply Day 5 concepts to your real role or studies
Build repeatable prompts you can reuse after the challenge
Practice safe, reviewable AI workflows
linkedinTemplate:
Day 5/60 of #60DayClaudeChallenge with @ABtalks 🚀

Today (Context Management): asked Claude to build me a phase-by-phase roadmap to become an AI Engineer. Realistic, weekly, and tailored to my level.

What’s the role you’d ask AI to map a path to? 👇

#60DayClaudeChallenge #ABtalks
solutionApproach: Submit a valid Claude share or artifact URL, plus your LinkedIn post URL when prompted.

dayContent.title: Context Management
dayContent.tagline: Use Claude as your personal mentor to build a real roadmap

learning.summary: Strong context = strong output. By telling Claude exactly who you are, what you want, and what your constraints are (time, current level, daily life), you get a realistic plan — not a generic listicle.

bullets.label: Frame the goal
bullets.text: give Claude your goal AND your constraints (time available, skill level, responsibilities).

bullets.label: Ask for phases
bullets.text: phases + milestones beat a flat 50-item to-do list.

bullets.label: Demand realism
bullets.text: instruct Claude to consider workload and avoid burnout — not just theory.

bullets.label: Make it actionable
bullets.text: always ask for the top 3 actions to start tomorrow.

task.title: Your Task
steps: Pick a real career goal (e.g. “become an AI Engineer”, “become a data scientist”).
Fill the mentor prompt below with your goal, your current level, and your weekly time budget.
Read the roadmap Claude returns — highlight the top 3 actions you’ll start this week.
Save the roadmap as a doc and share the headline phases on LinkedIn.

dayContent.promptTemplate:
Act as an expert mentor, strategist, and coach for a [student / working professional] who wants to achieve [goal].
Create a practical and realistic step-by-step plan while considering limited time, workload, current skill level, and daily responsibilities.

Include:
* Clear roadmap with phases and milestones
* Top priority actions to start immediately
* Weekly and monthly execution plan
* Important skills / tools / resources to learn
* Beginner-to-advanced project or practice ideas
* Productivity and time-management strategies
* Common mistakes to avoid
* Tips for consistency, growth, and avoiding burnout
* Career, portfolio, networking, and personal branding advice where relevant

Keep the response structured, actionable, beginner-friendly, and focused on real-world results rather than just theory.

engagement.description: Share the phase titles + the first 3 actions from your roadmap. Tag the role you’re building toward.

deliverable.description: A personalized career roadmap with phases, weekly plan, and first actions

tool.name: Caveman Skill
tool.description: Compress context into ultra-short, keyword-style notes and ask Claude to reply the same way — cuts tokens dramatically. Works in plain claude.ai chats; there’s also a one-click skill for Claude Code users.
tool.setupTitle: How to use the caveman technique
setupSteps: For everyone (no install): in any claude.ai chat, write your context in terse caveman style and add “reply in caveman style — keep all technical detail, cut filler words.”
Why it works: filler like “Sure, I’d be happy to help…” wastes tokens. Caveman keeps the facts and removes the fluff (often ~75% shorter).
For Claude Code users only: install as a one-line skill — run  claude install-skill JuliusBrussee/caveman  then trigger with  /caveman  or “caveman mode.”
Turn it off any time with “normal mode.”
Note: this is a free community skill, not an official Anthropic tool — the technique is what matters.
tool.linkLabel: View the caveman skill on GitHub

title: Day 6: Knowledge Checkpoint
problemStatement:
Welcome to **Day 6** of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

**Theme:** Phase 1 — Foundation & Prompt Engineering

**Today's focus:** A quick knowledge checkpoint. Five days in — let’s lock the basics before we accelerate.

**Tasks:**
1. Take the Day 6 MCQ on Days 1–5 concepts (released on abtalks.in).
2. Revisit any concept you got wrong using your own Claude chat.
3. Post one key thing you learned in the first week.

**Submission:** Share a Claude conversation link, artifact, or short doc that shows your work.

**Note:** Real content from our team launches before June 1, 2026.
learningObjectives: Apply Day 6 concepts to your real role or studies
Build repeatable prompts you can reuse after the challenge
Practice safe, reviewable AI workflows
linkedinTemplate:
Day 6/60 of #60DayClaudeChallenge with @ABtalks 🚀

Five days in — took the knowledge checkpoint today. The basics of prompt engineering are clicking.

What’s the one prompt habit that changed how you use AI? 👇

#60DayClaudeChallenge #ABtalks
solutionApproach: Submit a valid Claude share or artifact URL, plus your LinkedIn post URL when prompted.

dayContent.title: Knowledge Checkpoint
dayContent.tagline: Lock in the basics from Days 1–5

learning.summary: Today is a review day. No new technique — just consolidation. You’ll take a short MCQ on everything from setup through context management, then revisit the topics you got wrong.

bullets.label: Setup
bullets.text: Claude desktop install, Projects, and Artifacts.

bullets.label: Prompt Engineering
bullets.text: what it is and the normal vs engineered shift.

bullets.label: Role-Based Prompting
bullets.text: act-as personas, tone, and the “3 risks” trick.

bullets.label: Chain-of-Thought
bullets.text: step-by-step reasoning, verification, FINAL ANSWER.

bullets.label: Context Management
bullets.text: framing goals and constraints to get realistic, actionable answers.

task.title: Your Task
steps: Open the Day 6 MCQ on abtalks.in and complete it honestly (no Googling — it’s for you).
For every question you got wrong, open Claude and ask it to explain the concept again with an example.
Update your personal prompt log: which technique do you want to use more this week?
Post one thing you genuinely learned in the first 5 days.

dayContent.promptTemplate:
I just finished Days 1–5 of the 60-Day Claude AI Challenge: setup, prompt engineering, role-based prompting, chain-of-thought, and context management.

Quiz me with 5 mixed multiple-choice questions covering these topics. Make 2 of them tricky.
After my answers, give me:
1. Which I got right / wrong
2. A 1-line explanation of every concept I missed
3. One practical exercise to reinforce my weakest area.

engagement.description: Post one prompt habit you’ve built in the first 5 days that you wouldn’t have had a week ago.

deliverable.description: A short Week-1 reflection note with your weakest topic and how you’ll fix it

title: Day 7: ATS-Friendly Resume Building
problemStatement:
Welcome to **Day 7** of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

**Theme:** Phase 2 — Career & Communication

**Today's focus:** Build an ATS-friendly CV — fresher version, experienced version, or both.

**Tasks:**
1. Use the Fresher prompt if you’re a student/early-career, the Experienced prompt if you’re mid/senior.
2. Paste your real details (or rough notes) and let Claude rebuild your CV.
3. Tighten the result, export as PDF, and share it.

**Submission:** Share a Claude conversation link, artifact, or short doc that shows your work.

**Note:** Real content from our team launches before June 1, 2026.
learningObjectives: Apply Day 7 concepts to your real role or studies
Build repeatable prompts you can reuse after the challenge
Practice safe, reviewable AI workflows
linkedinTemplate:
Day 7/60 of #60DayClaudeChallenge with @ABtalks 🚀

Today (Resume Building): rebuilt my CV the ATS-friendly way — strong action verbs, measurable impact, recruiter-friendly format.

What’s the one section recruiters scan first on your resume? 👇

#60DayClaudeChallenge #ABtalks
solutionApproach: Submit a valid Claude share or artifact URL, plus your LinkedIn post URL when prompted.

dayContent.title: ATS-Friendly Resume Building
dayContent.tagline: A clean, keyword-rich CV that actually passes the scan

learning.summary: ATS (Applicant Tracking System) software scans your resume before any human sees it. Today you’ll learn to write a CV that survives the scan AND impresses the recruiter — with the right keywords, measurable impact, and clean formatting.

bullets.label: Strong action verbs
bullets.text: “Built, shipped, automated” — not “responsible for, helped with.”

bullets.label: Measurable impact
bullets.text: every bullet should show a number, percentage, or scale where possible.

bullets.label: ATS keywords
bullets.text: mirror the language used in real job descriptions for your target role.

bullets.label: Clean format
bullets.text: no tables, no images, no fancy fonts — simple wins.

task.title: Your Task
steps: Pick the right prompt for you below (Fresher or Experienced).
Paste your current resume content (or rough notes) into Claude with the prompt.
Refine the output: tighten weak bullets, add real numbers wherever you can.
Export the final version as a clean PDF and share a snippet on LinkedIn.

dayContent.promptTemplate:
=== FRESHER / STUDENT VERSION ===
Act as an expert resume writer and ATS optimization specialist.
Create an ATS-friendly one-page CV for a fresher/student targeting software engineering, AI/ML, or data science roles.
Highlight projects, internships, certifications, technical skills, and academic achievements using strong action verbs and measurable impact.
Include ATS-friendly keywords, a professional summary, clean formatting, and recruiter-friendly bullet points.
Keep the layout simple, modern, and optimized for internship and entry-level hiring.

=== EXPERIENCED / MID-SENIOR VERSION ===
Act as a senior resume strategist and ATS optimization expert.
Create a professional ATS-friendly CV for an experienced candidate targeting mid/senior-level roles in software engineering, AI engineering, or data science.
Emphasize work experience, leadership, technical expertise, business impact, achievements, and measurable results using quantified bullet points.
Include industry-relevant keywords, a strong professional summary, core competencies, project highlights, certifications, and ATS-safe formatting optimized for recruiter screening and high interview conversion.

engagement.description: Share a snippet of your new resume (blur the personal details) and one bullet you rewrote with measurable impact.

deliverable.description: An ATS-friendly one-page resume (Fresher or Experienced)

title: Day 8: Cold Outreach to Recruiters & Leaders
problemStatement:
Welcome to **Day 8** of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

**Theme:** Phase 2 — Career & Communication

**Today's focus:** Cold outreach on LinkedIn that actually gets replies — fresher or experienced angle.

**Tasks:**
1. Pick the target: a recruiter, hiring manager, or leader you genuinely want to reach.
2. Use the cold-outreach prompt to draft a personalized message.
3. Send 3 outreach messages this week and post the template (not the personal bits) for others.

**Submission:** Share a Claude conversation link, artifact, or short doc that shows your work.

**Note:** Real content from our team launches before June 1, 2026.
learningObjectives: Apply Day 8 concepts to your real role or studies
Build repeatable prompts you can reuse after the challenge
Practice safe, reviewable AI workflows
linkedinTemplate:
Day 8/60 of #60DayClaudeChallenge with @ABtalks 🚀

Today (Cold Outreach): wrote a LinkedIn outreach message that’s short, personal, and actually about *them* — not me.

What’s the one line that makes you reply to a cold message? 👇

#60DayClaudeChallenge #ABtalks
solutionApproach: Submit a valid Claude share or artifact URL, plus your LinkedIn post URL when prompted.

dayContent.title: Cold Outreach Mastery
dayContent.tagline: Short, personal, and about them — not you

learning.summary: Cold outreach works when you lead with something about the recipient, keep it under 80 words, and ask for one specific small thing. You’ll generate two flavors today: one for freshers (seeking guidance/internships) and one for experienced folks (seeking peer-level conversation or roles).

bullets.label: Them first
bullets.text: the first line must be about something specific the person did — not your introduction.

bullets.label: Under 80 words
bullets.text: if it doesn’t fit, it won’t get read.

bullets.label: One clear ask
bullets.text: ask for a 15-min call, a resource, or a single piece of advice.

bullets.label: Two follow-ups
bullets.text: if they don’t reply, follow up after 3 days, then 5 days later — with a new angle.

task.title: Your Task
steps: Pick one person you genuinely want to reach (recruiter, hiring manager, founder, mentor).
Use the Fresher or Experienced prompt below depending on where you are in your career.
Generate the message + 2 follow-ups. Personalize the first line manually — don’t leave it AI-generic.
Send it. Then post the reusable template (with personal details removed) so others can learn from it.

dayContent.promptTemplate:
=== FRESHER VERSION ===
Act as an expert LinkedIn outreach coach. Write a 3-message cold outreach sequence from a fresher/student to a [recruiter / hiring manager / leader] at [company].
Goal: [a 15-minute intro call / a referral / advice on breaking in].
Recipient context: [paste 1-2 lines about them — their role, a recent post, an achievement].
About me: [1 line — who I am, what I’m studying, the role I want].

Constraints: each message under 80 words; the first line must be about them, not me; one clear, polite ask only.

Output:
1. Initial message
2. Follow-up after 3 days (new angle, not a nag)
3. Final nudge after 5 more days (short, polite, easy yes)

=== EXPERIENCED VERSION ===
Act as an expert LinkedIn outreach coach. Write a 3-message cold outreach sequence from an experienced professional to a [hiring manager / VP / founder] at [company].
Goal: [a peer-level conversation / a mid-senior role conversation / a partnership chat].
Recipient context: [paste 1-2 lines about them and their work].
About me: [1 line — my current role, the unique value I bring, the role/conversation I want].

Constraints: each message under 80 words; the opener must reference something specific about them; one clear ask only.

Output:
1. Initial message (peer-to-peer tone, confident not desperate)
2. Follow-up after 3 days
3. Final nudge after 5 more days

engagement.description: Post the reusable version of your outreach template (no personal details). Add 1-2 lines on what made the personalization work for you.

deliverable.description: A 3-message cold outreach sequence (Fresher or Experienced)

title: Day 9: LinkedIn Profile Optimization
problemStatement:
Welcome to **Day 9** of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

**Theme:** Phase 2 — Career & Communication

**Today's focus:** Rebuild your LinkedIn profile so recruiters and peers actually find you.

**Tasks:**
1. Use the LinkedIn optimization prompt to rewrite your headline, About section, and top 3 experience bullets.
2. Add the keywords for the role you actually want.
3. Update LinkedIn and post a short note announcing the refresh.

**Submission:** Share a Claude conversation link, artifact, or short doc that shows your work.

**Note:** Real content from our team launches before June 1, 2026.
learningObjectives: Apply Day 9 concepts to your real role or studies
Build repeatable prompts you can reuse after the challenge
Practice safe, reviewable AI workflows
linkedinTemplate:
Day 9/60 of #60DayClaudeChallenge with @ABtalks 🚀

Today (LinkedIn Optimization): rebuilt my headline + About section so the right people can actually find me.

What’s the keyword you want to be found for? 👇

#60DayClaudeChallenge #ABtalks
solutionApproach: Submit a valid Claude share or artifact URL, plus your LinkedIn post URL when prompted.

dayContent.title: LinkedIn Profile Optimization
dayContent.tagline: Get found by the right people

learning.summary: Your LinkedIn profile is a search engine result. Today you’ll rewrite the three sections that matter most — headline, About, and top experience bullets — with the keywords for the role you actually want.

bullets.label: Headline
bullets.text: not your job title — a sentence that says what you do, who for, and the outcome.

bullets.label: About section
bullets.text: lead with the role you want, not your full history.

bullets.label: Experience bullets
bullets.text: rewrite the top 3 with measurable impact + ATS keywords.

bullets.label: Skills section
bullets.text: match the top skills in your target job postings, exactly.

task.title: Your Task
steps: Find 3 job postings for the role you want and list the repeated keywords.
Use the prompt below with those keywords + your current bio.
Update LinkedIn with the new headline, About, and top experience section.
Share a short post announcing the refresh and what you’re focused on next.

dayContent.promptTemplate:
Act as an expert LinkedIn profile strategist and personal branding coach.

My current LinkedIn:
* Headline: [paste current]
* About section: [paste current]
* Top role + 3 experience bullets: [paste current]

The role I want: [target role]
Keywords from 3 job postings: [paste keywords]
My biggest wins / proof points: [list 3-5 measurable wins]

Rewrite my profile to be ATS-friendly AND human-friendly:
1. A scroll-stopping headline (under 220 chars)
2. An About section that opens with the role I want and earns trust by line 3
3. Top 3 experience bullets rewritten with measurable impact and the right keywords
4. A list of 10 skills I should add and 5 I should remove

Make every line specific. No buzzwords.

engagement.description: Post a short note announcing your refreshed LinkedIn and what you’re focused on for the next 60 days.

deliverable.description: A LinkedIn rewrite document (headline, About, top experience, skills)

title: Day 10: Build Your One-Page Portfolio with Claude
problemStatement:
Welcome to **Day 10** of the 60-Day Claude AI Mastery Challenge.

**Theme:** Phase 2 — Career & Communication

**Today's focus:** Build a one-page portfolio website using Claude — fast, clean, and shareable.

**Tasks:**
1. Use the portfolio prompt to generate the full HTML+CSS for a one-page site.
2. Add your real projects, About, and contact.
3. Host it (GitHub Pages, Vercel, or Netlify) and share the live link.

**Submission:** Share a Claude conversation link, artifact, or short doc that shows your work.

**Note:** Real content from our team launches before June 1, 2026.
learningObjectives: Apply Day 10 concepts to your real role or studies
Build repeatable prompts you can reuse after the challenge
Practice safe, reviewable AI workflows
linkedinTemplate:
Day 10/60 of #60DayClaudeChallenge with @ABtalks 🚀

Today (Portfolio): built and hosted a clean one-page portfolio site with Claude. Going from “I should make one” to “it’s live” in a single sitting.

What’s the one project you’d feature on yours? 👇

#60DayClaudeChallenge #ABtalks
solutionApproach: Submit a valid Claude share or artifact URL, plus your LinkedIn post URL when prompted.

dayContent.title: One-Page Portfolio Site
dayContent.tagline: From idea to live link in one session

learning.summary: Today you’ll use Claude to generate a complete single-file portfolio site — hero, About, projects, contact — and host it on a free platform. No frameworks, no build step.

bullets.label: Single file
bullets.text: ask Claude for ONE index.html with inline CSS — easiest to host.

bullets.label: Strong hero
bullets.text: name + one-line value proposition + the role you want.

bullets.label: 3 projects max
bullets.text: each with a short outcome line. Quality over quantity.

bullets.label: Free hosting
bullets.text: drag-and-drop to Netlify or Vercel — live in under 60 seconds.

task.title: Your Task
steps: Decide what you want the site to say in one sentence (your value proposition).
List your top 3 projects with a 1-line outcome each.
Use the prompt below to generate the full single-file site.
Drag the file onto Netlify (or push to GitHub + Vercel) to get a live link. Share it.

dayContent.promptTemplate:
Act as a senior frontend engineer + designer.

Build me a single-file responsive one-page portfolio site (index.html with inline CSS, no frameworks, no external assets).

Style: dark theme, premium and modern (Linear / Vercel / Apple keynote vibe), bold sans-serif type, subtle gradient accents, generous whitespace, soft hover effects.

Sections:
1. Hero — my name [name], a one-line value prop: “[what I do for whom and the outcome]”, and a CTA button to my email.
2. About — 3 short paragraphs based on: [paste a few lines about you].
3. Projects — 3 cards. Each has: project name, 1-line outcome, link. Data: [paste 3 projects].
4. Contact — email + LinkedIn + GitHub links.

Constraints:
* Mobile responsive
* Smooth scroll, anchor nav
* Accessible color contrast
* Self-contained: I should be able to open the .html file and it just works.

Output ONLY the full HTML in one code block.

engagement.description: Post your live portfolio link. Tell us how long it took you from “I should build one” to “it’s live.”

deliverable.description: A live one-page portfolio site (hosted link + the index.html file)
